Jeff Grider, EdD
Keynotes and workshops on leadership, team performance, and getting out of your people’s way.
Download PDF

Dr. Jeff Grider is a Chief Operating Officer, educator, and performer who works at the intersection of operations and people. He holds a doctorate in Human Resource Education from the University of Illinois, an Executive Certificate through MIT, has led operations across specialty manufacturing and biotechnology, and spent years in instructional design and private consulting. He has also performed with elite vocal groups including Disney’s prestigious Voices of Liberty and the Home Free Vocal Band. Blending passion and purpose, the pulse of it all is understanding what it takes for capable people to perform at their best - which is usually less than one would think.

Signature Talks

Simplify. Elevate.
Capable people rarely underperform for lack of effort; they’re buried under process, noise, and complexity no one thought to question. Clear it away, and the performance was there all along.
Passion is a Privilege
The hourglass in action. The sand is everything life loads on you, gravity is time, and the narrow eye is awareness. Few reach it, and even fewer pass through. But those who do discover their passion on the other side, and it’s very hard to go back. Balancing what you love with what you do, even when they aren’t the same thing, makes you stronger in both.
Blend or Be Noise
Business and personal development lessons from performing at the highest level: listening over volume, blend over solos, and the trust that turns a room full of voices into a single instrument.
Be a Resource, Not a Threat
What the world of custom menswear taught me about influence and trust: give people a decision and they will make the choice; give them too many options and they will stall. How to guide people and teams toward values-driven decisions by becoming the resource they trust, not the threat they resist.

Audiences

Executive and leadership teams. Operations and manufacturing organizations. HR, talent, and L&D functions. Conferences and association events.

Formats

Keynote. Interactive workshop, half or full day. Breakout session. Panel or fireside. Leadership offsite facilitation.
